---
pagetitle: "Edward Hoffmann"
toc: false
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
---

::: {.hero}

# Edward Hoffmann

Economics and statistics (UC Davis, '25)

:::

## Bio {#bio}

I'm passionate about the theoretical foundations of decision-making and learning. My academic background includes graduate coursework in optimization, game theory, and classical ML.

Fun fact: I used to play a lot of Magic: the Gathering and was once interviewed by MTGGoldfish.

## Contact {#contact}

- **GitHub:** [github.com/edwardchoffmann](https://github.com/edwardchoffmann)
- **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/in/edward-hoffmann](https://www.linkedin.com/in/edward-hoffmann/)

## Interests {#interests}

### Research & technical

- Decision making under uncertainty: algorithmic game theory and reinforcement learning.
- Deep learning dynamics: structural and geometric properties of learning in neural networks (inspired by [Florentin Guth](https://florentinguth.github.io/publication/rainbow/), [David Noel Ng](https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys-ii/), and others).
- System dynamics & control: ergodicity, optimal control, and resource allocation.

### Hobbies

- Exercise
- Reading history and philosophy
- Cooking, gardening, and sewing
- Strategy games like Magic: the Gathering

## Projects {#projects}

### Fitting Hawkes processes on text-message data

Fitting and comparing Hawkes process specifications to message timestamps—testing which excitation structures best capture bursts, reply chains, and long-run rhythm in conversational data. *Nearly complete.*

### Upcoming {#upcoming}

### Learned geometry of “thinking” in transformers

Exploring how depth and residual structure shape internal representations during training—connecting ideas from [Florentin Guth](https://florentinguth.github.io/publication/rainbow/) on layer-wise dynamics with [David Noel Ng](https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys-ii/) on geometry and optimization in deep networks. *Planned / early stage (if I can run stuff locally without too many issues)*

### Random future ideas and potential projects

- Mess around with various RL architectures and use-cases
- MtG RL (the dream)
- Visual math exploration a la 3b1b about differential geomeotry, geodesics, and gradient descent
    - https://geomstats.github.io/index.html
- Jane Street puzzle solutions
- Some geospatial analysis (pretty but I already do this stuff at work so not currently something I want to work on in my free time so we'll see)
- Playing with signal processing/error correction methods
- Explore/play with an ergodic system
    - https://ergodicityeconomics.com/2023/10/20/an-ergodicity-perspective-on-reinforcement-learning/
- PINNs/Energy based model stuff
    - https://energy-based-model.github.io/Energy-based-Model-MIT/
    - Steve Brunton
